tasks.title: Seed thin CONTEXT.md for the claude-code-shared tooling repo
tasks.description: Create a CONTEXT.md at the claude-code-shared root containing a noun glossary (skill, agent, contract, hook, resource, registry, pipeline stage, tier, etc.) and load-bearing invariants (absolute-path script references, registry.json as source-of-truth for agents, model-tiers.json keying skills/agents by name, .cch/.cco symlinking into claude-code-shared, hooks wired via settings.json). This grounds the context-loader agent, which currently returns empty vocabulary/adrs for this repo.
acceptance_criteria: CONTEXT.md exists at claude-code-shared root and documents the noun glossary and invariants listed in the seed
Manually spawning context-loader against this repo returns non-empty vocabulary (verified by direct invocation, no unit test per infra convention)
tasks.pr: gh pr create --base main --head feat/improve-directory-structure --title "improve-directory-structure: seed CONTEXT.md" --body "See docs/tasks/20260709-1616-improve-directory-structure-skill.json T-0076"
tasks.summary: Created claude-code-shared/CONTEXT.md with a noun glossary (Skill, Agent, Contract, Hook, Resource, Registry, Pipeline stage, Tier) and load-bearing invariants (absolute-path scripts, registry.json as agent source of truth, model-tiers.json keying, .cch/.cco symlinking, hooks wired via settings.json, contracts gating agent I/O), verified by direct context-loader invocation returning 8 non-empty vocabulary terms.

tasks.title: Scanner analysis core: findings engine over nodes/edges/metrics JSON
tasks.description: Build the generic, repo-agnostic analysis core of the dependency-graph scanner in claude-code-shared/scripts/. It consumes a {nodes, edges, metrics} JSON document (fan-in, fan-out, dir depth, orphan flags) and computes findings against the curated litmus set: SRP (fan-out spans unrelated clusters), CCP/locality (edges point to one owner but node lives in shared), CRP (single-consumer resource bucketed as shared), DRY (high edge/content similarity between two nodes), ADP (cycle in the edge graph), SDP (churny high-fan-out node with many dependents), and a coupling/cohesion cluster-modularity score. Every finding must cite the principle and the specific graph edge/evidence proving the violation. Deterministic, tier-less script -- no extractor logic lives here.
acceptance_criteria: Script accepts a {nodes, edges, metrics} JSON fixture and emits a findings JSON with principle + evidence per finding

Running the script against a hand-built fixture containing one deliberate cycle, one deliberate DRY duplicate, and one deliberate misplaced-resource case surfaces exactly those three findings (verified by manual invocation, no unit test per infra convention)
tasks.pr: gh pr create --base main --head feat/improve-directory-structure --title "improve-directory-structure: scanner analysis core" --body "See docs/tasks/20260709-1616-improve-directory-structure-skill.json T-0077"
tasks.summary: Built claude-code-shared/scripts/dependency-graph-findings.py, the generic findings engine over a {nodes, edges, metrics} dependency graph, computing SRP/CCP/CRP/DRY/ADP/SDP/MODULARITY findings with cited evidence; verified manually against a hand-built fixture that surfaces exactly the deliberate cycle, DRY duplicate, and misplaced-resource cases with no false positives.

tasks.title: Claude-tooling edge extractor wired to the scanner core
tasks.description: Build the claude-tooling repo-type edge extractor: walks claude-code-shared and emits {nodes, edges, metrics} JSON by resolving registry.json entries, subagent_type= spawns, absolute script-path references, model-tiers.json keys, and skill-pipeline.json stage links into graph edges. Wire its output into the T-0077 analysis core so running the scanner end-to-end against the real claude-code-shared repo produces real findings, not fixture findings.
acceptance_criteria: Running `scanner --extractor claude-tooling` against claude-code-shared produces a nodes/edges/metrics JSON with no parse errors
Piping that output into the T-0077 core produces at least one real finding grounded in an actual edge in the current repo (verified by manual invocation, no unit test per infra convention)
tasks.pr: gh pr create --base main --head feat/improve-directory-structure --title "improve-directory-structure: claude-tooling extractor" --body "See docs/tasks/20260709-1616-improve-directory-structure-skill.json T-0078"
tasks.summary: Built claude_tooling_extractor.py (walks claude-code-shared and resolves registry.json consumers, subagent_type= spawns, absolute scripts/ path references, model-tiers.json keys, and skill-pipeline.json next[] links into a {nodes, edges, metrics} graph) and scanner.py (CLI dispatcher, --extractor claude-tooling). Verified end-to-end: 99 nodes/204 edges/no parse errors, piped through the T-0077 findings core to produce 137 findings, with two spot-checked findings confirmed against real, current-repo edges.

tasks.title: Generic-code edge extractor wired to the scanner core
tasks.description: Build the generic-code edge extractor: walks an arbitrary code directory and emits {nodes, edges, metrics} JSON by resolving import/require-style edges across common languages (JS/TS, Python at minimum). Wire its output into the T-0077 analysis core so the same scanner CLI can target a plain code repo, not just claude-code-shared.
acceptance_criteria: Running `scanner --extractor generic-code` against a sample JS/TS or Python directory produces a nodes/edges/metrics JSON with no parse errors
Piping that output into the T-0077 core produces findings consistent with the sample directory's known import structure (verified by manual invocation, no unit test per infra convention)
tasks.pr: gh pr create --base main --head feat/improve-directory-structure --title "improve-directory-structure: generic-code extractor" --body "See docs/tasks/20260709-1616-improve-directory-structure-skill.json T-0079"
tasks.summary: Built claude-code-shared/scripts/generic_code_extractor.py, a generic-code edge extractor that walks an arbitrary JS/TS/Python directory and emits {nodes, edges, metrics} JSON, and registered it as a second --extractor generic-code option in scanner.py's EXTRACTORS dispatcher alongside the existing claude-tooling extractor.

tasks.title: Scanner --check mode for reference-integrity validation
tasks.description: Add a --check mode to the scanner CLI that reuses the same edge-resolution pass built for T-0078/T-0079 to assert zero dangling edges and no new cycles, exiting non-zero on failure. Must follow .cch/.cco symlinks rather than treating them as ordinary edges, and must detect literal absolute-path string references (e.g. ~/.dotfiles/claude-code-shared/scripts/x.sh) in addition to structured registry entries.
acceptance_criteria: `scanner --check` against the current claude-code-shared repo exits 0 with no dangling edges reported

Deliberately introducing a dangling reference (e.g. a registry.json entry pointing at a deleted file) and re-running --check causes a non-zero exit that names the broken edge (verified by manual invocation, no unit test per infra convention)
tasks.pr: gh pr create --base main --head feat/improve-directory-structure --title "improve-directory-structure: scanner --check mode" --body "See docs/tasks/20260709-1616-improve-directory-structure-skill.json T-0080"
tasks.summary: Added scanner.py --check (reference-integrity mode) that reuses claude_tooling_extractor's edge-resolution helpers via a new check_integrity() function to surface dangling references (registry.json consumers/files, script refs, model-tiers/skill-pipeline keys, and literal absolute-path refs that correctly follow .cch/.cco symlink-farm aliasing) plus cycle detection via dependency-graph-findings.py, excluding intentional pipeline_next workflow loops.

tasks.title: Author improve-directory-structure SKILL.md
tasks.description: Write the improve-directory-structure skill: invokes the scanner (T-0077/T-0078/T-0079) to build the dependency graph, reuses the context-loader agent (now grounded by T-0076's CONTEXT.md) and the grill-step pattern to interrogate proposed reorg findings against the curated litmus set, and hands off a verified reorg plan via the to-seed handoff pattern. The skill stops at a verified plan/seed -- it must never execute file moves or reference rewrites directly; those become discrete tasks for the existing to-seed -> to-tasks -> build-code pipeline, each carrying scanner --check (T-0080) as its acceptance gate. Every reorg task emitted downstream must cite scanner --check as its acceptance gate. The skill must honestly scope itself to directories with structured, explicit cross-references and decline sparse/unstructured prose trees, mirroring how improve-codebase-architecture is scoped to module depth.
acceptance_criteria: SKILL.md exists at claude-code-shared/skills/improve-directory-structure/ and documents scope, litmus set, grill step, and verified-plan-only boundary

Running the skill end-to-end against claude-code-shared produces a verified reorg seed whose findings each cite a scanner edge and a litmus principle, and confirms no file moves were executed directly (verified by manual run, no unit test per infra convention)

receipt.summary: Authored improve-directory-structure SKILL.md + resources/LITMUS.md. Ran skill end-to-end (live, non-interactive) against claude-code-shared: scanner.py produced 101-node/209-edge graph, dependency-graph-findings.py produced 139 findings, 1 candidate (F0006) accepted and several declined against LITMUS.md, zero file moves executed. /to-seed handoff completed with full 4-persona + judge panel verification (5 refutations screened, 2 upheld as open threads ot-002/ot-003 on DRY/SRP bulk-decline grounding) -- seed status draft/verified, not blocked on further work for this task.
receipt.tests: no unit test per infra convention -- manual end-to-end verification recorded in trace log
